nodes.en: Culinary arts is the craft and science of preparing, cooking, and presenting food for consumption, encompassing technique, flavor composition, and aesthetic presentation.
nodes.cs: Kulinářské umění je řemeslo a věda přípravy, vaření a prezentace jídla ke konzumaci, zahrnující techniku, složení chutě a estetickou prezentaci.
nodes.brief:
Culinary arts is the craft and science of preparing, cooking, and presenting food for consumption. It combines technique — knife work, heat control, fermentation, emulsification — with flavor composition and visual presentation, so a dish functions simultaneously as nourishment, as a sensory experience, and, in its plating, as a small act of visual design.

Within [[06]], culinary arts is the subfield whose finished work is consumed and destroyed by its own use, unlike [[06.01]] or [[06.04]], whose objects persist after the making is done. It shares craft's emphasis on material fluency and hand skill with [[06.04]], and shares design's attention to composition and presentation with [[06.03]], while remaining distinct from [[06.02]] in that the "performance" of cooking is usually private, finished before the diner ever perceives it.

The [Culinary Institute of America](https://www.ciachef.edu/) and reference works such as [Harold McGee's food-science writing](https://curiouscook.com/) document technique and the underlying chemistry of cooking.
